goal: Approve registry/claude_codex_loop.py as an Apex tool by running TC-001..TC-009, completing docs/tools/claude_codex_loop/test_record.md with real evidence and the Approved YES stamp, adding the tool to APEX_TOOL_MENU.json + regenerating the menu + ensuring TOOL_INDEX.md + manifest.json reflect approval. (Register row already exists: APEX-MB-PY-00023.)
requester: Claude (Opus 4.7, GPU-RTX5090, doc author per operating contract)
assigned_to: Codex (code review + test execution + index/menu mutations per operating contract)

forbidden_actions: Promote any OTHER batch-#3..#5 artifacts in this packet — one batch at a time per playbook
Bulk-merge other parallel-Claude WIP files outside the claude_codex_loop scope
Edit claude_codex_loop.py beyond what TC failures demand (attach diff to Task Result if any edit needed)
Bypass authentication or restore API-key auth in the safe_env_for_* functions — these are deliberate safety scrubs
Run TC-006 (smoke loop) against a path that contains uncommitted user changes the user cannot afford to lose

input_files: registry/claude_codex_loop.py — APEX-MB-PY-00023, 386 lines, stdlib only, builder+reviewer loop
docs/DOCUMENT_REGISTER.md — claude_codex_loop already at row APEX-MB-PY-00023
docs/tools/claude_codex_loop/blueprint.md — drafted by Claude this packet
docs/tools/claude_codex_loop/guidance.md — drafted by Claude this packet
registry/validate_tool_docs.py — must still return OK after this batch
registry/generate_tool_menu.py — used to regenerate the human menu
write_scope: docs/tools/claude_codex_loop/test_record.md — REPLACE stub with real test evidence + Approved stamp
docs/APEX_TOOL_MENU.json — add entry under approved_tools[] with category 'ai' (or 'governance' if you prefer); use exact_call from blueprint
docs/APEX_TOOL_MENU.md — REGENERATE from JSON via generate_tool_menu.py (do not hand-edit)
docs/DOCUMENT_REGISTER.md — verify existing APEX-MB-PY-00023 row is correct (path apex/registry/claude_codex_loop.py, status ACTIVE). NO new row needed unless verification reveals drift.
registry/manifest.json — refresh entry to reflect approved state
registry/TOOL_INDEX.md — REGENERATE per approve_tool.py / indexer policy

audit/claude_codex_loop/SMOKE-001/ — natural side-effect of TC-006; commit only the final SIGNED_OFF.json or LOOP_FAILED.json + the changed_files manifest, NOT the full builder/reviewer transcripts (per playbook constraint)
hub/WO-APEX-PROMOTE-CLAUDE-CODEX-LOOP-002.json — append `status` field with COMPLETED/BLOCKED + completion timestamp + result link
constraints: Make the smallest safe change that completes the task — do NOT refactor the loop for style.
TC-005 (env scrub) is non-negotiable. Run it. Do NOT skip even if other TCs pass.
TC-006 (smoke loop) requires Claude CLI + codex_bridge.py both available. If either is missing on the test machine, file a BLOCKER, do not silently mark PASS.
Use distinct phase labels for any test runs (e.g. SMOKE-001, SMOKE-002) so prior runs aren't overwritten.
Idempotency: rerunning the promotion steps should not double-write the register row, menu entry, or manifest entry.
No secrets in any committed artifact — and DEFINITELY not in any audit transcript Codex commits.
Run validate_tool_docs --quiet after EACH menu/manifest edit, not just at the end.

files: docs/DOCUMENT_REGISTER.md (verified — likely no change since row exists)
audit/claude_codex_loop/SMOKE-001/SIGNED_OFF.json OR LOOP_FAILED.json (whichever is the deterministic outcome)
expected_output.report: TASK RESULT with: status (OK|BLOCKED|FAIL), all TC commands run with exit codes and log paths, validate_tool_docs final result, links to SIGNED_OFF/LOOP_FAILED evidence, risks remaining, rollback instructions, recommended next batch (#3 doctrine + policy).

test_command: python registry/validate_tool_docs.py --quiet && python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('registry/claude_codex_loop.py').read())" && python registry/claude_codex_loop.py --help > NUL
success_criteria: All 9 TCs in test_record.md have actual results (no remaining `_pending_`).
TC-005 (env scrub) explicitly verified: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDE_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY all absent from the returned env dicts even when set in the parent shell.
APEX_TOOL_MENU.json has a claude_codex_loop entry under approved_tools[].
APEX_TOOL_MENU.md regenerated from JSON (mtime newer than JSON).
DOCUMENT_REGISTER.md row for APEX-MB-PY-00023 is consistent with the current file path; if drift found, fix and note in the Task Result.

No secrets committed (run `python registry/muscle_compliance_check.py --quiet --strict` if batch #1 has already approved compliance_check; otherwise grep audit transcripts for `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY` to confirm clean).
rollback_plan: If approval cannot be completed: (1) git restore docs/APEX_TOOL_MENU.json docs/APEX_TOOL_MENU.md registry/manifest.json registry/TOOL_INDEX.md to undo registry mutations; (2) leave the four docs/tools/claude_codex_loop/*.md in place (they're harmless DRAFTs); (3) DO NOT delete `audit/claude_codex_loop/` evidence (existing or new); (4) append BLOCKED status + reason to this Task Packet; (5) escalate via Task Result `blocker_report_fields`. If the loop tool itself has a discovered regression, follow the rollback steps in docs/tools/claude_codex_loop/troubleshoot.md (disable schedules → keep evidence → git checkout prior sha).
evidence_required: Exact commands run for each TC plus stdout / exit code (paste into test_record.md per template format).
TC-005 env-scrub mechanism (code snippet + observed env dict contents).
TC-006 smoke loop final JSON path (SIGNED_OFF or LOOP_FAILED — either is acceptable; the test is that the LOOP terminates deterministically).
TC-009 linked SIGNED_OFF and LOOP_FAILED JSON paths with status confirmation.
Diff of any source edit beyond stub-to-real test_record edits.

stop_for: TC-005 env-scrub FAILS (one of the three keys leaks into a subprocess env)
TC-006 smoke loop hangs past timeout AND no log file written (loop is genuinely broken, not just slow)
Claude CLI not installed/authenticated on the test machine
codex_bridge.py not present at default path AND no override path available
validate_tool_docs.py returns non-OK after the menu/register edits
blocker_report_fields: Why it blocks promotion (vs. being a pre-existing unrelated FAIL)
Safe fallback path (e.g. mark tool DRAFT, do not add to TOOL_MENU until blocker resolved)
What can continue independently (batch #3 doctrine/policy is independent of this tool)

doctrine_notes: Per APEX_CONTEXT_INDEX.md operating contract: Claude owns architecture/docs/reviews; Codex owns code edits/tests/configs. This packet honors that split.

Per APEX_PARALLEL_MERGE_PLAYBOOK: 'Link only selected transcript evidence from `audit/claude_codex_loop/`; do not require future contexts to read the whole audit tree.' Test record TC-009 links exactly two transcripts, not the whole tree.
Per user instruction 2026-05-25 13:53Z: 'Do not self-review around Codex where Codex review is required.' Claude must NOT mark this tool APPROVED — the YES stamp is Codex's gate.

codex_result.result_summary: Codex fixed env scrub, negative-test log creation, and max-attempt terminal status. Live smoke and controlled cap tests ran. The invalid TC-009 failure evidence was replaced with AGEN-verification-agent/LOOP_FAILED.json, and the tool was promoted to the approved menu, manifest, and tool index.
codex_result.safe_next_action: Use only through the documented guidance and keep scheduled production use disabled unless a specific Task Packet authorizes it.
